The bill also caps TV gambling ads at three per hour during the day, bans stadium and jersey promotions, and requires social platforms to build an opt-out mechanism for adult users, though it faces an eight-week Senate inquiry.

title: Getty Plans to Terminate Shutterstock Merger Because of UK Regulations
blurb: Getty Images plans to terminate its $3.7B merger with Shutterstock on July 6 after the UK's Competition and Markets Authority demanded Shutterstock divest its editorial imaging business, a condition Getty's board unanimously refused. The collapse leaves both stock-photo agencies fighting generative-AI competition without the scale the deal promised, and Shutterstock shares fell roughly 30% on the news to sit down 48% year-to-date.

title: AI search visibility startup geoSurge scores £9.4m round
blurb: London-based geoSurge raised $12M (£9.4M) in an oversubscribed seed round led by AlbionVC to help brands shape how generative AI systems like ChatGPT, Gemini, and Claude internally represent them. The bet is that today's citation-tracking approach to AI visibility gets commoditized, so geoSurge targets the underlying data models with a "Corpus Engineering" framework rather than surface-level prompt monitoring.

title: Matching AI Modality To User Intent: Designing The Right Interface
blurb: The article argues that design teams have defaulted to the chat bubble for every AI feature, when modality should be chosen by matching the user's physical and cognitive load to the task. It offers two tools for the decision, a Task Audit and an Input/Output Alignment Matrix, that evaluate input and output modality separately. The practical takeaway for PMs: treat "should this even be a chat interface" as an explicit discovery question, using the airport-assistant failure case as the test for when typed input and dense text output break down at the moment of need.

title: Visa, Stripe among firms joining forces on mainstream stablecoin
blurb: Visa, Stripe, and a consortium of payments firms are partnering to launch a mainstream stablecoin aimed at consumer and merchant settlement, per Bloomberg. The move puts incumbent card networks in direct competition with existing issuers like Circle and Tether, whose combined supply now anchors most of the roughly $200B stablecoin market.

title: Ondo tokenizes BlackRock’s IVV ETF and Micron stock under US custodial model
blurb: Ondo launched tokenized versions of BlackRock's iShares Core S&P 500 ETF (IVV) and Micron shares on Ethereum, the first time a third party has tokenized US-listed securities on a public chain within existing US regulatory infrastructure rather than offshore. The tokens are minted 1:1 by Ondo's SEC-registered transfer agent under the SEC's January custodial framework, with holders retaining brokerage-equivalent rights including onchain proxy voting through Broadridge.

title: UK dilutes stablecoin capital requirement in final crypto rulebook
blurb: The UK softened stablecoin backing rules in its final crypto rulebook, allowing issuers to hold a portion of reserves in short-term government bonds rather than requiring full backing in central bank deposits. The change eases capital constraints for issuers like Circle and PayPal ahead of the regime's rollout, aligning the UK closer to EU and US frameworks that already permit government-debt reserves.

title: Securitize Tokenizes Its Own NYSE Stock SECZ on Its First Day as a Public Company
blurb: Securitize began trading on the NYSE under the ticker SECZ on its first day as a public company and simultaneously tokenized that same common stock on Avalanche and Solana, available to eligible KYC'd US investors through its own regulated platform. The RWA tokenization leader, which has more than $4B onchain across funds with BlackRock, Apollo, and KKR, says SECZ is set to become the world's largest tokenized stock at launch.

title: Securitize becomes first to debut shares on NYSE and onchain, but it won’t be the last
blurb: Securitize debuted its shares simultaneously on the NYSE and onchain, becoming the first company to list a traditional equity as a tokenized asset alongside its public offering. President Brett Redfearn said the tokenization firm is in talks to bring other IPOs onchain within the next year, signaling a template for pairing conventional listings with onchain settlement.

title: eToro leads $12.5 million round in onchain perps exchange Extended
blurb: eToro led a $12.5 million strategic round in Extended, an onchain perpetual futures exchange built on StarkWare's StarkEx scaling engine, with Jump Crypto also participating. The investment is tied to a partnership with Zengo, the multi-party-computation self-custody wallet eToro acquired earlier this year at a roughly $70 million valuation, as the broker extends its stack into onchain derivatives.

title: Warren Pushes to Bar Trump Family From Crypto Profits After $1.4B Disclosure
blurb: Senator Elizabeth Warren is pushing to add a provision to the Senate crypto market-structure bill that would bar Trump, his family, and senior officials from profiting off the digital asset industry, following a disclosure showing Trump's crypto ventures generated over $1.4 billion in his first year back in office. The provision enters active negotiation as the bill approaches a floor vote, adding a conflict-of-interest fight to legislation the industry expects to define US market structure.

title: CFTC’s Selig says Illinois lawmakers ‘decided they know better’ on crypto tax
blurb: CFTC Chair Michael Selig criticized Illinois for enacting a 0.2% tax on crypto transactions, saying lawmakers "slammed the brakes on technological progress" and diverged from federal efforts to clarify digital asset rules. The Digital Asset Tax Act, signed by Governor JB Pritzker as part of the FY2027 budget, takes effect in January 2027 and has drawn industry pushback as the most punitive state crypto tax.

title: Knowledge Over Parameters: Evolving Smart Contract Vulnerability Detection
blurb: EvoVuln reframes smart contract vulnerability detection as a procedural-knowledge evolution problem, synthesizing and refining detection logic from as few as five vulnerable and five safe examples per bug type. It decouples deterministic control flow from LLM reasoning and evolves rules without parameter updates, hitting a 71% macro-average F1 across five vulnerability types. The evolved knowledge is portable, letting a small model beat a larger zero-shot one by 19 points for under $50.

title: Failed blockchain project ends with big fine for fibs about it being on track
blurb: US regulators fined the operators of a collapsed blockchain venture for repeatedly telling investors the project was on schedule when it had already stalled. The penalty adds to a pattern of enforcement targeting misleading progress claims rather than the underlying technology, signaling that overstated development timelines carry the same liability as any other securities misrepresentation.

title: Anchorage Digital adds Lido support, giving institutions access to wstETH
blurb: Anchorage Digital added support for Lido, giving its institutional clients direct access to wstETH with the ability to mint and burn the token through the Lido dApp while retaining Anchorage's custody and governance controls. The integration brings liquid staking to a US federally regulated platform and could lift Lido revenues, which fell over 20% in 2025 as users withdrew funds and staking yields declined.

title: eToro Takes Strategic Stake in Onchain Derivatives Exchange Extended, Plans Zengo Tie-Up
blurb: eToro took a strategic stake in Extended, a Starknet-based perpetual futures decentralized exchange, as part of a $12.5M round reported by CoinDesk, and plans to pair the venue with Zengo, the self-custody wallet it acquired in April for roughly $70M. The tie-up would let eToro offer onchain perpetuals across Extended's 100-plus crypto, equities, FX, and commodities markets to wallet users who retain custody of their assets.

title: Passive Income on Ethereum for All: How Rocket Pool Scales Liquid Staking
blurb: Rocket Pool's Saturn 1 upgrade expands the liquid staking protocol's scaling capacity, tightens rETH's peg to ETH, and restructures RPL staking into an ETH-denominated yield strategy. In an interview with Camila Russo, GM Darren Langley framed the changes as making rETH more capital-efficient for node operators while turning RPL from a collateral requirement into a source of real staking returns.

title: Resilient Liquid Democracy: Mitigating Voting Power Imbalances via Secure Delegation Networks
blurb: This paper proposes a secure liquid democracy mechanism that hides delegation choices during voting formation using decentralized timed-release encryption, preventing herding and coercion while keeping the final tally publicly auditable. It adds ranked multi-delegation and personal fallback ballots to survive delegate abstention. Tested on four real datasets including 60,000 US voters, the authors find delegation only improves accuracy when abstention is large and unrepresentative, and that sealed formation mainly reduces voting-power concentration rather than boosting accuracy.

title: ENS DAO Sunsets Public Goods Working Group After 4.5 Years of Ecosystem Grants
blurb: ENS DAO sunset its Public Goods Working Group after 4.5 years, with a final term that committed $450,000 in USDC and 72.5 ETH to grantees including Vyper, Solidity maintainer Argot Collective, and Remix Labs. The closure lands amid a broader governance and treasury restructuring, including a temp-check vote to hand authority to the ENS Foundation, and no replacement funding pipeline has been announced.

title: Bitget expands Stock+ platform with US stock options trading
blurb: Bitget launched U.S. stock options trading for eligible users, adding single-leg long call and long put positions to its Stock+ platform alongside existing tokenized stocks and pre-IPO access. The Seychelles-based exchange plans to add multi-leg strategies later, joining Coinbase and Binance in pushing equity and options products as U.S. options volume hit a record 15.2B contracts in 2025.

title: Securing People and their Machines Against Major Faults
blurb: This paper tackles account recovery for grassroots platforms, decentralized systems where people are identified by self-chosen public keys on their smartphones with no global authority to fall back on. Its peer-based scheme uses a social graph of friendships, person-designated identity custodians, and platform-specific state custodians, so a supermajority of custodians can approve a key rotation and let friends propagate the new key across the graph. The authors prove their message-passing implementation recovers from lost keys or wiped devices without double-spending grassroots coins.

title: Peter Thiel-backed crypto-friendly Erebor Bank eyes $8 billion valuation as deposits nearly quadruple: Bloomberg
blurb: Erebor Bank, the crypto- and defense-focused startup founded by Palmer Luckey and backed by Peter Thiel, is in talks to raise at a valuation of at least $8 billion, roughly double the $4.35 billion it secured in December, according to Bloomberg. Deposits have nearly quadrupled since March to $4.05 billion, and Erebor, which holds a national bank charter and plans dollar stablecoin deposits, expects to be profitable by year-end.

title: JPMorgan says Strategy’s bitcoin sale policy introduced ‘avoidable’ risk into crypto markets
blurb: JPMorgan analysts said Strategy's newly formalized BTC Monetization Program, which lets the company sell up to $1.25B in bitcoin to fund dividends and buybacks, introduced "avoidable two-way risk" by making the largest holder of ~4% of BTC supply a potential seller. The analysts recommended Strategy issue common equity to lift cash reserves from 17 months to 24-36 months of dividend coverage.

title: US bitcoin ETFs break 10-day negative streak with $222 million worth of inflows
blurb: US spot bitcoin ETFs recorded $222 million in net inflows on Thursday, snapping a 10-day stretch of outflows. BlackRock's IBIT bucked the trend as the sole fund to post net redemptions, shedding $40.4 million and extending its own negative streak.

title: Samsung, Dunamu say they were listed as OUSD stablecoin consortium members without official consultation: report
blurb: Samsung Electronics, Dunamu, Shinhan Financial Group, and Kbank said they never agreed to join Open Standard's OUSD stablecoin consortium, telling Chosun Biz they were listed as members without formal consultation and some learned of their inclusion from news reports. The disputed roster undercuts a project that claims Visa, Mastercard, BlackRock, and 140-plus participants ahead of a planned launch within the year.

title: Securitize Tokenizes Its Own NYSE Stock SECZ on Its First Day as a Public Company
blurb: Securitize began trading on the NYSE under the ticker SECZ and tokenized its own newly listed common stock on Avalanche and Solana the same day, making it the first newly public company to bring its stock onchain at launch. Eligible U.S. investors can access tokenized SECZ through Securitize's regulated platform after KYC and AML checks, with the token representing the same shares rather than a synthetic wrapper.

title: Ondo tokenizes BlackRock’s IVV ETF and Micron stock under US custodial model
blurb: Ondo Finance launched tokenized versions of BlackRock's iShares Core S&P 500 ETF (IVV) and Micron shares on Ethereum, the first time a third party has tokenized US-listed securities on a public blockchain under the SEC's January custodial framework. Ondo's SEC-registered transfer agent Oasis Pro TA mints tokens backed 1:1 by shares held in the conventional US custody chain, with holders retaining full shareholder rights including onchain proxy voting through Broadridge.

title: Robinhood Chain Mainnet Goes Live With Stock Tokens
blurb: Robinhood launched Robinhood Chain, its own Arbitrum-based Layer 2, on public mainnet, alongside Stock Tokens that track equities 1:1 with broker-held shares and can be posted as collateral in DeFi lending pools across 120+ countries. The tokens are unavailable in the US, where Robinhood is still waiting on an SEC framework for tokenized equity after submitting a 42-page proposal last year.

title: Securitize becomes first to debut shares on NYSE and onchain, but it won’t be the last
blurb: Securitize debuted its shares simultaneously on the NYSE and onchain, becoming the first company to list a public equity across both traditional and tokenized rails. President Brett Redfearn said the firm is already in talks to tokenize other IPOs within the next year, signaling that dual listings could become a standard on-ramp for bringing public equities onto blockchain settlement.

title: Warren Pushes to Bar Trump Family From Crypto Profits After $1.4B Disclosure
blurb: Senator Elizabeth Warren is pushing to add a provision to the Senate crypto market-structure bill that would bar Trump, his family, and senior officials from profiting off digital assets, after a disclosure showed his crypto ventures generated over $1.4B in his first year back in office. World Liberty Financial and Trump-branded meme coins drove the bulk, escalating an ethics fight already entangling the bill's floor vote.

title: eToro leads $12.5 million round in onchain perps exchange Extended
blurb: eToro led a $12.5 million strategic round in Extended, an onchain perpetual-futures exchange built on StarkWare's StarkEx, with Jump Crypto also participating. The investment is tied to a partnership with Zengo, the self-custody wallet eToro acquired earlier this year, and signals the broker's push to connect its brokerage stack to onchain trading infrastructure.

title: CFTC’s Selig says Illinois lawmakers ‘decided they know better’ on crypto tax
blurb: CFTC Chair Michael Selig publicly attacked Illinois for enacting a 0.2% tax on crypto transactions, saying lawmakers "slammed the brakes on technological progress" and diverged from federal efforts to build market-structure clarity. The Digital Asset Tax Act, signed by Governor Pritzker in the state's FY2027 budget, takes effect January 2027 and has drawn industry claims that it is the most punitive digital-asset tax in the country.

title: eToro Takes Strategic Stake in Onchain Derivatives Exchange Extended, Plans Zengo Tie-Up
blurb: eToro became a strategic investor in Extended, a Starknet-based onchain perpetuals exchange, in a round CoinDesk pegged at $12.5M, and paired it with Zengo, the self-custody wallet eToro bought in April for roughly $70M. Combining Zengo's custody with Extended's cross-asset derivatives engine lets eToro offer onchain perps to users who keep control of their assets, mirroring Robinhood's push into onchain product suites.

title: Anchorage Digital adds Lido support, giving institutions access to wstETH
blurb: Anchorage Digital, a federally regulated crypto bank, added support for Lido's liquid staking protocol, giving institutional clients direct access to mint and burn wstETH within Anchorage's custody and governance controls. The integration brings liquid-staked Ethereum exposure to large allocators without the validator-infrastructure overhead, and could help reverse Lido's revenue slide after staking-derived income fell more than 20% in 2025.

title: China Quant Funds Draw Billions as AI Trounces Human Traders
blurb: Chinese quantitative funds are pulling in billions in new capital as AI-driven strategies consistently outperform human traders, fueling a surge in inflows that managers are struggling to absorb. The rush concentrates China's retail and institutional money into algorithmic firms, raising crowding risks as similar models chase the same signals and regulators weigh tighter oversight of automated trading.

title: Travel app Hopper to pay $35M in FTC settlement over ‘unfairly’ charging hidden fees
blurb: Hopper agreed to pay $35M to settle FTC charges that it used dark patterns to hide fees and pre-select optional charges for its VIP Support, Tip, and Price Freeze services. The settlement bars Hopper from misrepresenting pricing and requires upfront disclosure of all fees, extending an FTC enforcement pattern that has already hit Match, StubHub, and neobank Dave.

title: Bitget expands Stock+ platform with US stock options trading
blurb: Bitget launched U.S. stock options trading for eligible users, adding long call and long put positions to its Stock+ platform alongside existing tokenized stocks and pre-IPO access. The initial release covers single-leg options buying with multi-leg strategies planned later, and mirrors similar equity-product expansions from Coinbase and Binance as crypto exchanges push deeper into traditional-market trading.

title: Hong Kong Rises as Crucial Node in Asia’s $2 Trillion AI Trade
blurb: Hong Kong has emerged as a critical transit hub in a $2 trillion network of Asian AI-hardware trade, routing high-tech chips and components in and out of mainland China. The city's role as a gateway sharpens the compliance stakes for banks and payments firms financing the flows, given US export controls and the scrutiny that trade attracts from FinCEN and OFAC.
